name: Chili Poblano Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the Crema Fresca ahead of time: Mix Cream and Sour Cream together. Cover and let stand at room temperature until thickened, 8 hours or overnight. Chill until ready to use.
2. Char the Chili Peppers over a gas flame until blackened on all sides. Wrap them in a plastic bag and let stand for 10 minutes to steam. Peel and core the Chilies. Remove seeds, rinse, and pat dry.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
4. Generously butter a 9-inch pie pan (preferably porcelain or stoneware with 2-inch sides).
5. Open up Chili Peppers and arrange around sides of pan skin side down, pointing toward the center of the pan, extending about 1/2 inch above the rim.
6. Cover bottom of pan with Chilies.
7. Finely grate cheese with Onion and garlic in a processor using on/off pulses for about 30 seconds.
8. Add Eggs and Salt. Process until smooth, stopping to scrape down the sides, about 15 seconds.
9. Mix in the Crema Fresca (the Mexican equivalent of creme fraiche).
10. Pour this filling over the Chilies.
11. Curl the edges of the Chilies over the filling.
12. Bake until golden brown and a knife inserted in the center comes out clean, 45 to 50 minutes.
13. Cover Chili edges with foil if necessary to prevent burning.
14. Cool for 5 minutes before cutting. Serve hot or at room temperature.
